name: Clifford Jordan
abstract: Clifford Laconia Jordan (September 2, 1931 – March 27, 1993) was an American jazz tenor saxophone player and composer. Originally from Chicago, Jordan later moved to New York City, where he recorded extensively in addition to touring across both Europe and Africa.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Randy Weston Sextet
Hubert Laws Afro-Classic Ensemble/ Randy Weston Quintet
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
New Jazz: John Blake and Stanley Cowell
JVC Jazz Festival: Dizzy Gillespie Big Band: An Evening of Mostly Monk

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I was on call at Riverside, and I was still recording on an average of 4 or 5 dates sometimes a week... My friend Clifford Jordan got an album that I'm really crazy about... very happy to have been on that date.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I was with his big band for a few years, playing the baritone, along with playing the tenor and alto in that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Clifford Jordan came into the band... We had been working together years before with Horace Silver’s group... I started calling Clifford, and that was a very great relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I used to go down there on the weekends and just listen... everybody that came through town came there afterwards, from Rahsaan Roland Kirk, Clifford Jordan... And I also used to go there and play... if I remember correctly, it was my father and maybe Clifford Jordan might have been there
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: the reason why Pharaoh Sanders’ recording landed with Strata-East was because of the great Clifford Jordan... he said, “Hey, here you go, run with it.”

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I got to meet people like Hank Mobley, Kenny Dorham, and played with all these people I'd heard and played with on records. Clifford Jordan
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
